helpful: Full petition and BIO text made the vehicle/posture arguments assessable directly

fedcourts query on granted 2020s priors confirmed the multi-distribution pattern preceding grants
gaps: The prompt references a per-Term 'Segment base rate by salience band' statpack section, but this statpack revision does not carry it, so the salience-band anchor could not be used
This case's own salience score/band is not exposed in the provisioned cell, only on corpus prior rows
A cases-citing-authority search (petitions raising a given precedent) would help gauge how often an overrule campaign like this one has been denied before
notes: Petition text search on SCOTUS dockets via MCP returns nothing (petition bodies are not indexed), so amicus/QP signals had to come from the provisioned documents; that provisioning worked well here.
